LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EVON CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - PER ORDER OF THE PRESIDING JUDGE, THE DEMURRER IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 23, 2014, TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE GOLDSMITH. JUDGE MILLER IS HEARING THE CALENDAR AND RECUSES HERSELF. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-23-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER OVERRULING DEFENDANT EVON CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT EVON CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES STANDING TO MAINTAIN THIS ACTION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT HE IS A SHAREHOLDER OF DEFENDANT AT PARAGRAPHS 3 AND 11-12 OF THE COMPLAINT AND DEFENDANT FAILED TO TRANSFER THE SHARES. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY WARRANTING DECLARATORY RELIEF UNDER CCP 1060. LASTLY, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE WHETHER DEFENDANT ACTED IN ACCORDANCE WITH OR VIOLATED THE CORPORATIONS CODE. DEFENDANT ARGUES THAT DEFENDANT WAS INCORPORATED IN 1967 AND AS A FACTUAL MATTER OUTSIDE OF THE PLEADINGS, DEFENDANT NEVER FILED AN AMENDMENT OF ITS ARTICLES WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE ELECTING TO BE GOVERNED BY THE 1977 AMENDMENTS. EVEN IF THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE CORPORATIONS CODE SECTIONS CITED IN THE COMPLAINT ARE INAPPLICABLE TO THIS ACTION. EXHIBIT A OF THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE REFERS TO AN ENTITY NAMED "EVON GARAGE CORPORATION." THE DEFENDANT IN THIS CASE IS NAMED "EVON CORPORATION." EXHIBIT B OF THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS A PRINT OUT FROM THE SECRETARY OF STATE'S WEBSITE. THE COURT CAN TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE WEBSITE, BUT CANNOT ACCEPT THE CONTENTS OF THE WEBSITE AS TRUE. SEE SEARLES VALLEY MINERALS OPERATIONS, INC. V. STATE BD. OF EQUALIZATION (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 514, 519 (AFFIRMING DENIAL OF REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF MATERIALS CONTAINED ON THE WEBSITE PAGES OF THE AMERICAN COAL FOUNDATION AND THE U.S. DEPARTMENT OF ENERGY); RAGLAND V. U.S. BANK NAT'L ASS'N (2012) 209 CAL.APP.4TH 182, 193 (WHILE A COURT "MAY TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE EXISTENCE OF [A GOVERNMENT] REPORT, WEBSITES, AND BLOGS, [IT] MAY NOT ACCEPT THEIR CONTENTS AS TRUE."). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SEAN M. SELEGUE OF ARNOLD & PORTER LLP, TEL. NO. 471-3100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EVON CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff alleges standing to maintain this action. Plaintiff alleges that he is a shareholder of defendant at paragraphs 3 and 11-12 of the complaint and defendant failed to transfer the shares. Plaintiff alleges an actual controversy warranting declaratory relief under CCP 1060. Lastly, the court cannot determine whether defendant acted in accordance with or violated the Corporations Code. Defendant argues that defendant was incorporated in 1967 and as a factual matter outside of the pleadings, defendant never filed an amendment of its articles with the Secretary of State electing to be governed by the 1977 amendments. Even if the court grants defendant's request for judicial notice, defendant fails to show that the Corporations Code sections cited in the complaint are inapplicable to this action. Exhibit A of the request for judicial notice refers to an entity named "Evon Garage Corporation." The defendant in this case is named "Evon Corporation." Exhibit B of the request for judicial notice is a print out from the Secretary of State's website. The court can take judicial notice of the website, but cannot accept the contents of the website as true. See Searles Valley Minerals Operations, Inc. v. State Bd. of Equalization (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 514, 519 (affirming denial of request for judicial notice of materials contained on the website pages of the American Coal Foundation and the U.S. Department of Energy); Ragland v. U.S. Bank Nat'l Ass'n (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 182, 193 (while a court "may take judicial notice of the existence of [a government] report, Websites, and blogs, [it] may not accept their contents as true."). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2014 Continued To Sep-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
